evidence: All 7 services report status:healthy. P1:65MB, P2:90MB, P3:56MB, P4:43MB, Exec:80MB, Cross-Chain:107MB

actual: Leader elected with healthy lock TTL

title: stream:opportunities at MAXLEN cap — potential message loss
evidence: XLEN=10001 (MAXLEN=10000). Pending=44 for coordinator-group. stream_health_status=0.5 (warning). Producers (4 partitions) generating ~15K+ opportunities in 150s. Coordinator consuming but older unread messages may be trimmed.
actual: At cap — approximate trimming may discard unread messages
recommendation: Increase MAXLEN for opportunities to 50000, or increase coordinator consume rate. Consider consumer lag alerting when pending > 80% MAXLEN.

title: Coordinator has no /metrics or /stats endpoint
evidence: curl http://localhost:3000/metrics returned empty. curl http://localhost:3000/api/stats returned 'No stats endpoint'. Only /api/health and /api/leader available.
expected: Prometheus metrics endpoint for stream health, forwarding rates, consumer lag
recommendation: Add /metrics endpoint to coordinator exposing forwarding rate, opportunities processed, validation rejections, consumer lag

title: Cross-chain detector has no /metrics endpoint
recommendation: Add /metrics endpoint for cross-chain opportunity detection rate, chain pair coverage

title: Coordinator consumer lag acceptable — 33-44 pending on opportunities stream
evidence: T0: pending=44, T1 (15s later): pending=33. Coordinator is processing and ACKing. price-updates pending=17-23.

title: DLQ coordinator-group has 0 consumers — dead consumer group
evidence: XINFO GROUPS stream:dead-letter-queue shows coordinator-group with consumers=0, pending=0
actual: 0 consumers — group exists but no consumer attached
recommendation: Verify coordinator registers a DLQ consumer during startup. If DLQ processing is deferred/lazy, document this behavior.

title: Two unexpected consumer groups found
evidence: orderflow-pipeline on stream:pending-opportunities (consumers=1, pending=0). failover-coordinator on stream:system-failover (consumers=1, pending=0). Neither listed in System Inventory.
actual: 7 consumer groups including orderflow-pipeline and failover-coordinator
recommendation: Update System Inventory to include these groups. orderflow-pipeline likely from FEATURE_ORDERFLOW_PIPELINE; failover-coordinator from cross-region health.

title: Idle stream health metrics show 0 (critical) for intentionally idle streams
evidence: stream_health_status{stream=swap-events}=0, stream_health_status{stream=whale-alerts}=0, stream_health_status{stream=volume-aggregates}=0. These are [IDLE] streams with no producers wired.
expected: Health metric should distinguish IDLE from DEAD
actual: Shows critical (0) for all idle streams — misleading in dashboards
recommendation: Add stream lifecycle label (active/idle/on-demand) to health metric so alerts can exclude intentionally idle streams

title: Pipeline latency 3ms p99 — well under 50ms target
evidence: pipeline_latency_p50_ms=3, pipeline_latency_p95_ms=3, pipeline_latency_p99_ms=3. Counter: 39 events in ~180s.

title: Risk state NORMAL — trading allowed at full capacity
Execution engine 0 opportunities received — coordinator forwarding very selectively
evidence: opportunitiesReceived:0, executionAttempts:0. stream:execution-requests has 1 entry (from startup). execution-engine-group pending=0.
actual: 0 executions — coordinator validation is filtering all simulated opportunities
recommendation: Check coordinator forwarding criteria. In simulation mode with high realism, verify that simulated opportunities can pass coordinator validation thresholds. Consider logging forwarding rejections for visibility.

title: All services within memory thresholds
evidence: P1:65MB, P2:90MB, P3:56MB, P4:43MB, Exec:80MB, Cross-Chain:107MB. All well under 512MB constrained limit.
